message.content:
The wind holds its breath in the pine-scented air,
A hush falls heavy on the valley’s stare.
The sun, a pale coin sinking low and slow,
Paints gold upon the peaks of winter’s snow.
The chimney stacks exhale a gray, thin thread,
A final sigh before the night is spread.

Then comes the first, a single, drifting speck,
A ghostly mote against the dusk’s dark deck.
It dances down, a silent, slow ballet,
To kiss the roof where shadows start to play.
Another follows, then a dozen more,
Unlocking secrets from the frozen door.

The village sleeps beneath a woolen shroud,
Where silence wears a crown of softest cloud.
The cobblestones, once rough with rain and tread,
Are smoothed by layers of the sky’s white bed.
The church bell tower, stark against the gray,
Is wrapped in lace of intricate display.

No crunch of boots, no barking dog’s sharp cry,
Just feathers falling from a hollow sky.
The world is muted, wrapped in cotton wool,
As night begins to spin its silver spool.
The trees stand still, their branches bowed and bent,
With heavy gifts of ice they cannot spend.

Inside the cottages, the firelight glows,
A warm, orange pulse behind the frosted glass.
The villagers dream of rivers’ flow,
While outside, time itself seems to pass
In slow, white motion, quiet and profound,
The earth is gently, beautifully bound.

By morning’s light, the world is new-born clean,
A diamond landscape, sharp and bright and keen.
The first snowfall has stitched the sky to land,
Holding the mountain village in its hand,
A perfect stillness,
